headline: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features | TechCrunch
article_title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features | TechCrunch

article_items.title: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.
article_items.excerpt: Google has released Android 17 and Wear OS 7, introducing new multitasking features, parental controls, security tools, and smartwatch upgrades. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.
article_items.summary: The latest release, which arrives first on its own Pixel devices, is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop , bringing new features that include support for the latest AI models, like the music-generation model Lyria 3, the multimodal Gemini Omni, and speech-to-translation tools for the Pixel 10a with AudioLM. While its rival Apple is focused on catching up in AI with September’s public launch of AI upgrades to Siri and iOS 27, Google’s Android 17 is focused on its newest AI models, Gemini’s role in creation. Beyond AI, Android 17’s larger update allows users to take advantage of features like a “bubble bar,” which is a new user interface element that lets you organize, move, and then quickly access recent apps that appear as bubbles at the bottom of your screen. The feature is designed to help speed up app interactions and aid in multi-app workflows. Meanwhile, watch owners can now receive live updates from phone apps that mirror to the Pixel Watch. The launch is also accompanied by a Pixel Drop that brings Google’s latest AI models to its devices.

article_items.title: Introducing container caching in Amazon SageMaker AI for faster model scaling | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: Today, we’re excited to announce container image caching for Amazon SageMaker AI inference, the next major advancement in our faster scaling optimization journey. This speeds up end-to-end latency by up to 2x for generative AI models during scale-out events.
article_items.excerpt: Today, we’re excited to announce container image caching for Amazon SageMaker AI inference, the next major advancement in our faster scaling optimization journey. This speeds up end-to-end latency by up to 2x for generative AI models during scale-out events.
article_items.summary: Artificial Intelligence Introducing container caching in Amazon SageMaker AI for faster model scaling Today, we’re excited to announce container image caching for Amazon SageMaker AI inference, the next major advancement in our faster scaling optimization journey. Faster scaling on new instances (this launch): Container cache removes image pull when launching new instances, reducing the end-to-end scaling latency by up to 50 percent. Amazon SageMaker AI previously introduced sub-minute Amazon CloudWatch metrics to help detect scale-out needs up to 6x faster than traditional mechanisms and launched an inference component data caching solution that stores container images and model artifacts on already running instances. article_items.title: LangSmith: Redesigned product homepage and Resource Tags for better organization
article_items.description: LangSmith's homepage is now organized into Observability, Evaluation, and Prompt Engineering. Learn why we organized the homepage like this. Plus, see our latest Resource Tags updates.
article_items.excerpt: LangSmith's homepage is now organized into Observability, Evaluation, and Prompt Engineering. Learn why we organized the homepage like this. Plus, see our latest Resource Tags updates.
article_items.summary: One of the biggest recommendations for AI engineers is to “look at your own data” - and LangSmith enables this by helping users identify issues and understand their LLM application’s performance over time. S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click. LangSmith enables user to store and version prompts , making it easy to track their evolution and collaborate seamlessly with team members. Dashboards allow you to create custom monitoring charts to track the metrics that matter the most to your application over time. The Playground also allows for side-by-side comparisons of how different prompt and model combinations perform.

LangSmith: Redesigned product homepage and Resource Tags for better organization Today, we’re excited to release a redesigned LangSmith product homepage as well as improved support for resource organization within your.
These updates reflect our ongoing insights into how engineering teams build, optimize, and iterate on their LLM applications.
Earlier this year, we introduced Resource Tags to enhance resource grouping and accessibility.
This section in LangSmith includes Prompts and the Playground.
LangSmith enables user to store and version prompts , making it easy to track their evolution and collaborate seamlessly with team members.
Additionally, the Playground in LangSmith allows users to iterate on prompts in real-time.
Users can import existing data, or automatically add data points from their logs in the Observability section of LangSmith.
In this post, we will walk through our updated beliefs and how they guided our design.
Effective prompt engineering is necessary for building a responsive and reliable LLM application.

article_items.title: Context Management for Deep Agents
article_items.description: Learn how Deep Agents SDK manages context for long-running AI tasks through offloading, summarization, and filesystem abstraction to prevent context rot.
article_items.excerpt: Learn how Deep Agents SDK manages context for long-running AI tasks through offloading, summarization, and filesystem abstraction to prevent context rot.
article_items.summary: Deep Agents implements a filesystem abstraction that allows agents to perform operations such as listing, reading, and writing files, as well as search, pattern matching, and file execution. S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click. The most insidious failure mode is an agent that loses track of the user's intent after summarization. For example, by forcing the agent to summarize frequently, we could identify how simple changes to the deepagents summarization prompt, in which we added dedicated fields for the session intent and next steps, help improve performance. This process has two components: In-context summary: An LLM generates a structured summary of the conversation - including session intent, artifacts created, and next steps - which replaces the full conversation history in the agent's working memory. article_items.what_to_watch: This process has two components: In-context summary: An LLM generates a structured summary of the conversation - including session intent, artifacts created, and next steps - which replaces the full conversation history in the agent's working memory. By triggering compression at 25% of the context window (rather than.
key_points: As the session context crosses 85% of the model’s available window, Deep Agents will truncate older tool calls, replacing them with a pointer to the file on disk and reducing the size of the active context.
By triggering compression at 25% of the context window (rather than the Deep Agents default of 85%), we generate more events to study.
When Deep Agents detects a tool response exceeding 20,000 tokens, it offloads the response to the filesystem and substitutes it with a file path reference and a preview of the first 10 lines.
Deep Agents implements a filesystem abstraction that allows agents to perform operations such as listing, reading, and writing files, as well as search, pattern matching, and file execution.
To manage context limits, the Deep Agents SDK triggers these compression steps at threshold fractions of the model's context window size.
The Deep Agents SDK is LangChain’s open source, batteries-included agent harness .

(Under the hood, we use LangChain's model profiles to access the token threshold for a given model.) Offloading large tool results Responses from tool invocations (e.g., the result of reading a large file or an API.
(See the Deep Agents summarization prompt .) Filesystem preservation: The complete, original conversation messages are written to the filesystem as a canonical record.

Context Management for Deep Agents As the addressable task length of AI agents continues to grow , effective context management becomes critical to prevent context rot and to manage LLMs’ finite memory constraints.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.
All features of the Deep Agents harness are open source.

article_items.title: Agent Engineering: A New Discipline
article_items.description: Learn how agent engineering combines product thinking, engineering, and data science to build reliable LLM systems. Ship faster with iterative testing.
article_items.excerpt: Learn how agent engineering combines product thinking, engineering, and data science to build reliable LLM systems. Ship faster with iterative testing.
article_items.summary: The organizations shipping reliable agents today are extending the skills of engineering, product, and data teams to meet the demands of non-deterministic systems. To achieve a reliable agent system, shipping is how you learn, not what you do after learning. Software engineers and ML engineers writing prompts and building tools for agents to use, tracing why an agent made specific tool calls, and refining the underlying models Platform engineers building agent infrastructure that handles durable execution and human-in-the-loop workflows Product managers writing prompts, defining agent scope, and ensuring the agent solves the right problem Data scientists measuring agent. Ship your improvements and watch what’s changing in production. What is agent engineering?

What is agent engineering?
The organizations shipping reliable agents today are extending the skills of engineering, product, and data teams to meet the demands of non-deterministic systems.
To achieve a reliable agent system, shipping is how you learn, not what you do after learning.

A new standard for engineering The teams shipping reliable agents today share one thing: they've stopped trying to perfect agents before launch and started treating production as their primary teacher.
An agent can have 99.99% uptime while still being off the rails and broken.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.
Agent Engineering: A New Discipline If you’ve built an agent, you know that the delta between “it works on my machine” and “it works in production” can be huge.

Building systems (evals, A/B testing, monitoring etc.) to measure agent performance and reliability Analyzing usage patterns and error analysis (since agents have a broader scope of how users use them than traditional.
The faster you move through this cycle, the more reliable your agent becomes.
Agent engineering is emerging because the opportunity demands it.
Why agent engineering, and why now?

article_items.title: Introducing Open SWE: An Open-Source Asynchronous Coding Agent
article_items.description: Open SWE is an open-source, cloud-hosted coding agent that autonomously handles GitHub tasks - planning, coding, testing, and opening PRs.
article_items.excerpt: Open SWE is an open-source, cloud-hosted coding agent that autonomously handles GitHub tasks - planning, coding, testing, and opening PRs.
article_items.summary: Introducing Open SWE: An Open-Source Asynchronous Coding Agent The use of AI in software engineering has evolved over the past two years. S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click. In addition to the LangGraph framework, Open SWE is deployed on LangGraph Platform (LGP). Tech We Used: LangGraph and LangGraph Platform Open SWE is built on LangGraph, which allows us to have more control over each step in the workflow. We believe the future of software development will be collaborative between humans and agents. So we built Open SWE , the first open-source, async, cloud-hosted coding agent.

Open SWE is an open-source, cloud-hosted coding agent that autonomously handles GitHub tasks - planning, coding, testing, and opening PRs.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.

Tech We Used: LangGraph and LangGraph Platform Open SWE is built on LangGraph, which allows us to have more control over each step in the workflow.
We think UI/UX is often the one of the more under-explored areas in agent building.

LGP is purpose built for long running agents (these agents can sometimes run for an hour at a time), comes with built in persistence (powering our human-in-the-loop features) and autoscales (so that we can kick of.
For tasks like these, you want the agent to be able to skip the planning and reviewing stages, and jump right into execution.
How It Works: The Agent Architecture Open SWE operates using three specialized LangGraph agents that work in sequence: a Manager, a Planner, and a Programmer (which contains a sub-agent Reviewer).
Deeply integrated: Open SWE integrates directly with your GitHub account and repositories, so you can assign it work like any other developer and give it context to your code.

key_points: Daimon Robotics and Galbot jointly launches RobOmni for benchmarking tactile perception and dexterous manipulation GENISOM AI debuts deployable robotics platforms at ICRA 2026 GMSL and the growing ecosystem around.
The San Carlos, Calif.-based company last year raised $105 million in seed funding toward development of general-purpose robotics.
Genesis AI launches Eno general-purpose robot Genesis AI today unveiled Eno, its general-purpose robot.

Eno is an important step forward for what general-purpose robots can help society achieve, and Eno is just the beginning.” Genesis plans to begin Eno’s production and targeted customer deployments by the end of 2026.
Genesis plans to begin Eno’s production and targeted customer deployments by the end of 2026, with home deployments coming later.
“From Day 1, we’ve approached our design and engineering through a production mindset built around bringing our hardware, software, and intelligence together as a whole.
Eno is powered by GENE, the company’s foundation model.
In real-world environments, this enables the robot to move beyond simple actions and instead manage entire workflows, from keeping production lines stocked to preparing facilities for the next shift.
GENE empowers Eno’s hands with human-level dexterous manipulation to allow the robot to perform complex, long-horizon tasks with millimeter precision.

“We reduced the form to its essential elements so that every detail serves a purpose, from the way it moves between environments to the precision, range, and sensitivity required to operate alongside people in the real.
They match the form and function of human hands, so they can easily interact with the tools, objects, and environments already designed with humans in mind.

“The only path to creating a robot that can truly deliver value to society and excel in the real world is through intentional design and a single, comprehensive system,” said Zhou Xian, co-founder and CEO of Genesis AI.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Separate proven capability from claimed capability.
